summary_strategy: Position Conner as a builder-operator who scales secure cloud platforms from zero-to-scale, emphasizing shipped production systems, compliance-as-automation (HIPAA/SOC 2/HITRUST), and operational reliability in high-stakes environments
skills_strategy: Use condensed 3-line format to maximize relevance; lead with Security Engineering & Automation, then Cloud/Platform/Integrations (highlighting Azure-adjacent experience via AWS/GCP + Microsoft Graph), and finally Security Data/Analytics/AI to mirror JD’s emphasis on CSPM/SIEM and AI governance

experience_focus.must_highlight: HIPAA-aligned guardrails, Azure-adjacent cloud operations (Microsoft Graph API), CI/CD automation with secure-by-default patterns
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices, 25 percent compliance improvement, 200 hours saved annually, 30 percent reduction in user-reported issues
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: Any mention of 'HIPAA' as a direct responsibility unless explicitly stated in source; avoid claiming ownership of audit outcomes or certification attainment without evidence

risk_controls: Replace all 'HIPAA-aligned' with 'compliance-driven' or 'audit-ready' unless source explicitly states HIPAA; never invent compliance frameworks beyond those cited in resume
Never state 'built X for Y compliance'—instead say 'translated compliance requirements into deployable controls'
Avoid passive phrases like 'helped support' or 'contributed to'; use active verbs: 'built', 'engineered', 'architected', 'implemented'
Remove all speculative metrics unless directly quoted in source (e.g., keep '200 hours saved' only if tied to specific tooling at Great Wolf)
